title: Nozzle Theory & Isentropic Flow
summary: The nozzle converts thermal energy into directed kinetic energy. Explore choked flow, the area-Mach relation, expansion ratio design, and how back-pressure creates shock diamonds or plume expansion.

keyEquations.label: Area–Mach Relation
keyEquations.description: Relates local area ratio to Mach number for isentropic flow

keyEquations.label: Thrust Coefficient
keyEquations.description: Dimensionless thrust. Momentum + pressure thrust terms.

keyEquations.label: Isp from c* and CF
keyEquations.description: Specific impulse is the key figure of merit

objectives: Derive exit Mach from nozzle expansion ratio using the area-Mach relation
Calculate Isp (vacuum and sea-level) and understand the effect of ambient pressure
Classify flow regimes: ideally-expanded, under-expanded, over-expanded
Design optimum expansion ratio for a target altitude
Visualize property gradients (Mach, pressure, temperature) inside the nozzle
